sources.label: Investing.com - Neo Performance Materials Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (beat + raised guidance, May event)
Neo Performance Materials - Q1 2026 results press release
TipRanks - Neo starts heavy rare earth separation at Estonia Silmet plant (April announcement)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already marked it down for the post-rerating chase (+80% YTD on a $1.05B cap), and no new fundamental information has emerged since June 6. Today's -4.85% dip is flow-driven profit-taking with the thesis intact, which marginally improves asymmetry but does not undo the rerating, so hold at #33 with a small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: Only Western-integrated rare-earth-magnet platform with Europe's first sintered magnet plant ramping through customer qualifications in 2026; Q1 EPS of $0.36 crushed estimates and full-year EBITDA guidance was raised to $100-110M, so the robotics/motor supply-chain thesis is being validated by actual numbers.
verdicts.neutral: The fundamental story is real but is now well-discovered: +80% YTD has priced in most of the magnet-onshoring narrative, and a -4.85% no-news day suggests fast money rotating rather than a thesis change either way.
verdicts.bear: China's pause of export controls (until Nov 2026) deflates the scarcity premium that powered the rerating; if magnet pricing normalizes or the Estonia commercial ramp slips, an 80% YTD gainer has a long way to give back.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Commercial ramp and customer qualification wins at the Estonia permanent magnet plant in H2 2026, plus any new Western OEM magnet supply contracts.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: post-rerating multiple compression if China keeps export controls paused and rare-earth scarcity pricing fades.
